bundleDelta.note: Module total delta +3.97 KB (G-001 baseline 24.64 → G-006 final 28.61). 30 KB limit honored with 5.36 KB headroom remaining for MOD-GRID-03~16 future Goals on grid-core.

moduleObservations: MOD-GRID-02 state area = 6 Goals comprising the GridState abstraction: G-001 useGridState integration / G-002 controlled mode + initialState / G-003 onStateChange aggregator + debounce / G-004 resetState/resetSection/clearSelectionKey / G-005 useUrlSync helper / G-006 useStoragePersist helper. All 6 PASS thresholds.

Score pattern: 100/93.75/100 (specify/implement/verify) repeated identically for G-005 + G-006 — both low-tier helper Goals with A-07 vitest-runtime NO as the single deduction. G-001~G-004 (medium-tier core) all 100/100/100 except G-003 specify 97.5 (which was an absent-JSON artifact, not real defect). Calibration appears consistent — implement A-07 NO is the only systematic deduction (3 occurrences in module: G-003/G-004/G-005 + 4th occurrence G-006 = environment infra, not implementer defect).

Hook-only pattern across all 6 Goals: every Goal has affectedUsageFiles=0 (no tw-framework-front pages migrated). verify D category vacuous=100 in every Goal (6 occurrences = solid pattern, not anecdote). MOD-GRID-17 (사용처 채택) deferred — D category will become real-scoring only there.

C-32 option 3 (eslint-disable for non-stable internal hook callable) cumulative count after module completion: G-004 useControllableState setValue (1st) + G-005 useDebouncedCallback raw fn (2nd) = 2 occurrences (pattern entry). G-006 added 0 (Option A saveRef pattern successfully prevented 3rd occurrence). MOD-GRID-99-B/internal-hook-callable-stability promoted-Goal pending — recommended to execute before next module that uses internal hooks (MOD-GRID-04 column, MOD-GRID-08 advanced features etc.).

Bundle efficiency variance (vs goals.json D9 estimates): G-001 +0.12 KB (6% of +2 KB est), G-002 +0.23 KB (11.5% of +2 KB est), G-003 +0.88 KB (88% of +1 KB est), G-004 +1.49 KB (149% of +1 KB est — over estimate but within 30 KB hard limit), G-005 +1.05 KB (105%), G-006 +0.32 KB (32%). ADR-MOD-GRID-00-010 hook-profile matrix now has 6 data points within single module — extrapolation-blocking rule empirically solid.

AC-005 binding consistency (Storybook + vitest): G-004/G-005/G-006 all spec Section 7 enumerate `*.stories.tsx` + `*.test.ts`. G-005 introduced E-01 v1.0.6 binding-cross-check (file-level enumeration), G-006 applied it successfully. R-1 cascading risk (C-30 ↔ AC-binding loophole) from G-004 is now structurally resolved at specify-rubric level — no longer cascades to MOD-GRID-03+.
moduleLevelPatterns: Pattern-M1 (saveRef envelope): G-005 used hydrateRef pattern (onHydrate non-stable). G-006 extended with saveRef pattern (debouncedSave non-stable). Both rely on `useRef + always-sync useEffect (no deps)` to bypass C-32 option 3 without eslint-disable. Useful catalog entry for future internal-hook + consumer-callable patterns across MOD-GRID-03+ (column hooks, virtualization callbacks, edit-mode commit callbacks).

Pattern-M2 (URLSearchParams envelope reuse): G-005 produced `internal/serializeState.ts`. G-006 reused it (zero modification) as JSON envelope payload (`{v: version, p: params.toString()}`). DRY pattern that allows future MOD-GRID modules to serialize grid state to ANY transport (URL, storage, IndexedDB, postMessage) via the same envelope.

Pattern-M3 (mount-only useEffect with explicit eslint-disable on `[]` deps): All hook Goals (G-005, G-006) use this for one-time hydration. This is the only eslint-disable category remaining (intentional mount-only) — distinct from C-32 option 3. Catalog as 'safe eslint-disable pattern'.

R-1_vitestInfraGoalPromotion.evidence: G-005 self-review newCascadingRisks R-1: 3 occurrences A-07 NO (G-003/G-004/G-005). Applied APPLY-1 in G-005: implement-rubric v1.0.9 environment deviation rule. G-006 implement-score.json L130 failedChecks=['A-07'] = 4th occurrence chronologically, but rule v1.0.9 now provides documentedDeviations[] + toolingBacklog TB-2 pathway. Implementer applied v1.0.9 pathway correctly — A-07 NO retained for transparency (not converted to N/A), 6 test scenarios written + typecheck PASS, deviation documented. Rule operates as designed. **No new improvement** — v1.0.9 working. However, vitest infra Goal still UNCOMPLETED — backlog item TB-2 remains pending across 4 Goals. Promotion priority should escalate: from 'recommended' to 'high priority before MOD-GRID-03 starts'.
R-1_vitestInfraGoalPromotion.subFinding: G-005 self-review proposed Goal ID `MOD-GRID-99-B/G-NNN-vitest-infra`. **Naming consistency check**: Goal ID should be canonical — recommend `MOD-GRID-99-B/G-001-vitest-infra` (first Goal in 99-B test-infra module) for ease of tracking. Self-Review APPLY-3 below.

R-2_internalHookStableCallables.evidence: G-005 self-review newCascadingRisks R-2: C-32 option 3 = 2 occurrences (G-004 useControllableState + G-005 useDebouncedCallback). G-006 spec Section 4.3 + D2 explicitly defined Option A (saveRef pattern) to prevent 3rd occurrence. Implementer applied D2 correctly: useStoragePersist.ts L127-130 saveRef + L135-146 saveRef.current() call with state primitives deps. C-32 option 3 occurrences added by G-006 = **0** (verified — implement-verifier-score.json c32UnstableSetterPass + implement-score.json B-05 evidence). Cumulative count remains 2 (G-004 + G-005). 3rd-occurrence-block rule functioning correctly. **No new improvement** — broad reading rule (constraints.md C-32 L476-484) successfully prevented 3rd occurrence. Promoted-Goal (`internal-hook-callable-stability`) still recommended but not blocking.
R-2_internalHookStableCallables.subFinding: G-005 self-review proposed Goal ID `MOD-GRID-02/G-006-internal-hook-stable-callables`. **Naming conflict**: G-006 is now USED for `useStoragePersist` Goal (this Goal). The proposed Goal ID is therefore stale/invalid. Self-Review APPLY-2 below — re-name to `MOD-GRID-00/G-NNN-internal-hook-callable-stability` (cross-cutting infra) or `MOD-GRID-99-A/G-NNN-...` if a 99-A infra module exists.

R-3_hookOnlyVacuousTruth.evidence: G-005 self-review marked R-3 'persists-but-mitigated' — 2 anecdote occurrences at that point. After module completion, G-001~G-006 all hook-only Goals with affectedUsageFiles=0 → 6 verify D-category vacuous=100 instances within single module. **Pattern is now solid (6 occurrences)** but ADR-MOD-GRID-00-002 vacuous truth rule is intentional policy (infrastructure/hook Goals merit normal scoring; real D-category evaluation deferred to MOD-GRID-17 usage-site adoption). 1=anecdote, 2=pattern, 3=policy rule reaches threshold but **direction of policy change is DOWNWARD** (vacuous→non-vacuous would damage G-001~G-006 retroactively) — NOT recommended. The pattern is a CORRECT artifact of phased development (hooks-first, usage-sites-later). Policy reaffirmed, not changed. **REJECT** any rule change. **Documentation update only**: add module-end note to ADR-MOD-GRID-00-002 stating 'MOD-GRID-02 produced 6 vacuous-D occurrences across module; rule operates as intended for phased rollout.'

findings: F1 (cascading from G-005, resolved at G-006): R-2 (C-32 option 3 3rd-occurrence risk) successfully blocked. G-006 D2 Option A (saveRef + always-sync useEffect) eliminated need for eslint-disable on save-trigger useEffect. C-32 cumulative count unchanged (2). Spec writer (sonnet) introduced D2 design proactively after reading G-005 self-review — broad reading rule visibility working.

F2 (spec template defect F-06-G006-1, F-06 rule 2nd occurrence in module): G-006-spec.md Section 11.1 L546-547 code stub included `// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` on save-trigger useEffect. But D2 explicitly stated 'eslint-disable 0줄'. Spec writer included it as defensive over-specification (template inertia from G-005 useUrlSync.ts L95 pattern). Implementer (sonnet) applied 'tight read' — saveRef.current is a React Ref, react-hooks/exhaustive-deps auto-excludes refs without eslint-disable needed. Result: useStoragePersist.ts L100 has 1 eslint-disable (mount-only `[]`), save-trigger has 0. specCodeDefects[F-06-G006-1] recorded in both implement-score.json + implement-verifier-score.json. F-06 rule (v1.0.7, G-003 introduction) working as designed across module: G-003 useDebouncedCallback Rules-of-Hooks (1st) + G-005 `as any` cast (2nd) + G-006 over-specified eslint-disable (3rd). 3 occurrences = policy threshold. **Pattern observation**: spec writer (sonnet) tends to copy-paste between consecutive Goal specs as templates, occasionally introducing residual over-specification. Catalog item only (not policy change).

F3 (modules-level reflection — pattern catalog opportunity): G-005 + G-006 both introduce the `useRef + always-sync useEffect (no deps)` pattern (hydrateRef, saveRef). This is a reusable abstraction for 'consumer-supplied non-stable callable / value reference'. Worth considering: extract to `internal/useLatestRef.ts` in a future Goal (G-NNN-useLatestRef-extract) for use across MOD-GRID-04 column callbacks, MOD-GRID-13 advanced selection callbacks, etc. **Improvement candidate** — APPLY at low priority (anecdote→pattern at 2 occurrences, but extraction is optimization not correctness).

F4 (calibration check — score pattern uniformity): G-005 + G-006 produced identical scores 100/93.75/100. Both used same rubric versions (specify 1.0.6, implement 1.0.9, verify 1.0.2), same tier (low), same A-07 single NO. This is consistent calibration, not score collapse. Failed item A-07 is environmental (vitest absence), not implementer defect — penalty correctly attributed. Confidence: HIGH that future low-tier helper Goals will produce identical signature until A-07 environment resolved.

F5 (G-005 toolingBacklog entries — Goal ID re-naming needed): G-005 self-review appliedToolingBacklog cited two Goal IDs: TB-1 `MOD-GRID-99-B/G-NNN-vitest-infra` (still valid, anonymous-ID OK) and TB-2 `MOD-GRID-02/G-006-internal-hook-stable-callables` (**INVALID — G-006 is now occupied**). Self-Review must re-name TB-2 to a valid ID. Recommended: `MOD-GRID-00/G-NNN-internal-hook-callable-stability` (cross-cutting infra module) or alternatively `MOD-GRID-99-A/G-NNN-...`. APPLY in this self-review (APPLY-2 below).

F6 (module completion observation — affectedUsageFiles=0 across all 6 Goals): No tw-framework-front page migrated to use useGridState/useUrlSync/useStoragePersist yet. This is expected per MOD-GRID master plan (hooks-first phase). However, **wiring audit gap**: when MOD-GRID-17 (usage-site adoption) executes, every page migration will simultaneously test G-001~G-006 in production runtime. If a latent defect exists in any state hook, it will manifest at usage-site adoption — high blast radius. Mitigation: AC-005 vitest scenarios in G-001~G-006 should be RUN before MOD-GRID-17 entry. This is a stronger argument for the TB-1 vitest-infra Goal — escalate priority.

F7 (no new cascading risk discovered — module pattern stable): Reading G-001 through G-006 self-reviews + verify-scores end-to-end, I find no risk category not already covered by R-1/R-2/R-3 from G-005. The 'hook-only Goal' phase pattern is stable. New risks will emerge at module-boundary (when MOD-GRID-03 starts on column area, which has different concerns — column resize, reorder, visibility — and will test whether MOD-GRID-02 patterns scale).

F8 (1-anecdote-rule discipline maintained): Throughout the module, several improvement candidates were considered (vacuous truth rule weakening, F-06 expansion, broad/narrow reading codification, narrow-reading escape closure). All were either REJECTED or deferred to 3rd-occurrence threshold. This is correct discipline — no over-engineered rubric inflation. Module end with rubric versions: specify 1.0.6 (stable since G-005), implement 1.0.9 (G-005 introduced A-07 env deviation), verify 1.0.2 (stable since module start). Healthy evolution rate: ~3 rubric updates per 6 Goals.

newCascadingRisks.description: TB-1 (vitest-infra) and TB-2 (internal-hook-callable-stability) Goals remain UNCREATED at module completion. Cascading to MOD-GRID-03 (column area) means: (a) every new hook Goal in MOD-GRID-03 will continue accumulating A-07 NO (5th, 6th occurrences ahead) under v1.0.9 rule — score impact 0 but rubric-noise grows; (b) every new hook in MOD-GRID-03 that has an internal-hook non-stable callable risks C-32 3rd occurrence, which would auto-block the Goal per constraints.md C-32 (3rd occurrence → findings/blocked/ auto-written).
newCascadingRisks.mitigation: Escalate both backlog Goals to 'next-module-blocker' priority. Recommend: before MOD-GRID-03 Phase 1 (discover) starts, execute (1) TB-1 vitest-infra Goal (1 Goal, ~2 hours, install vitest + jsdom + testing-library + vitest.config.ts + scripts.test update + run G-001~G-006 existing test files) and (2) TB-2 internal-hook-callable-stability Goal (1 Goal, ~3 hours, useControllableState setValue useCallback memoize + useDebouncedCallback ms<=0 branch stable return + G-001~G-006 wiring re-verify + eslint-disable cleanup). Total ~5 hours. Module-boundary is the natural cleanup point.

newCascadingRisks.description: Spec writer (sonnet) introduced over-specification in G-006-spec.md Section 11.1 (eslint-disable on save-trigger that wasn't needed per D2). This is the 3rd occurrence of F-06-classified spec internal code defect within MOD-GRID-02 (G-003 Rules of Hooks, G-005 `as any`, G-006 over-specified eslint-disable). Common shape: spec writer (sonnet) inertia from prior Goal spec templates introduces stale/over-defensive code into new Goal spec. Implementer (sonnet) catches via 'tight read' but reliance on Implementer catch is fragile — if Implementer is rushed or copy-pastes literally, defect propagates.
newCascadingRisks.mitigation: F-06 rule (v1.0.7) is functioning. No rule change needed (3 occurrences = pattern, not yet policy). **Catalog the pattern** in findings/auto-fixed/MOD-GRID-02-spec-writer-template-inertia.md (or similar): document the 3 instances + recommend spec writer prompt include explicit 'review Section 11 against D# pre-decisions before finalize' checklist step. APPLY-4 below as documentation-only mitigation.

Catalog only.
